title: Probability & Stochastic Processes Attack: Δd=2 in Stochastic Calculus

expectation.measure_introduced: Probability measure P (the law of X)
expectation.conjecture_check: YES — E[·] = ∫ dP is precisely adding the probability measure
expectation.note: E[X]: rv(EML-0) → expectation(EML-2) = Δd=2; measure P introduced

ito_calculus.measure_introduced: Wiener measure W on C([0,T]) — the canonical path-space measure
ito_calculus.conjecture_check: YES — Wiener measure is introduced by the stochastic integral
ito_calculus.note: Itô: f(EML-1) → ∫f dB(EML-2) = Δd=1; but rv(0) → ∫f dB(2) = Δd=2

expectation_operator.key_insight: E[·] = canonical Δd=2: adds probability measure P; Itô = Δd=1 from EML-1

malliavin.note: Malliavin D = Δd=0 (EML-2→EML-2 self-map on Wiener space)

wick_rotation.measure_change: Oscillatory measure e^{iS} → damping measure e^{-S}
wick_rotation.note: Wick rotation: EML-3 → EML-1 = Δd=-2 (depth REDUCTION, not increase)

feynman_kac.note: Feynman-Kac: PDE(3) → stochastic rep(2) = Δd=-1 (reduction); existence=EML-∞

eml_depth_summary.delta_d_positive_2: E[·] from rv: adds probability measure P (canonical Δd=2)
eml_depth_summary.delta_d_positive_1: Itô integral ∫f dB: from EML-1 f
eml_depth_summary.delta_d_zero: Malliavin derivative D (self-map on Wiener chaos)

key_theorem: The EML Stochastic Calculus Theorem (S215): The expectation operator E[·] = ∫ dP is the canonical POSITIVE Δd=2 mechanism: random variable X (EML-0) → E[X] (EML-2): Δd=2 via probability measure P. Wick rotation and Feynman-Kac are NEGATIVE Δd operations (depth reductions). This reveals the BIDIRECTIONALITY of the Δd=2 measure theorem: adding a probability measure (E[·]) raises depth by 2; removing oscillation (Wick rotation) reduces depth by 2. The 'adding a measure' conjecture applies to both directions: ±2.
rabbit_hole_log: E[·] = canonical Δd=+2: every expectation adds the probability measure P
Wick rotation = Δd=-2: removes oscillatory measure, replaces with damping — bidirectional!
Feynman-Kac = Δd=-1 reduction: stochastic representation is simpler than PDE
